name: Parque Lineal de Plaza — circular along the Canal Imperial de Aragón
name_original: Parque Lineal de Plaza — circular junto al Canal Imperial de Aragón
region_intro: Touring cycling around Zaragoza offers a blend of urban and natural environments, with routes along the Ebro River and into the surrounding countryside. The region features extensive cycling infrastructure, including dedicated bike lanes and greenbelts. Landscapes range from lush riverbanks and unique river meanders like the Galacho de Juslibol to more arid areas and distant hills. This diverse terrain provides varied touring cycling experiences for different skill levels. Best touring cycling routes around Zaragoza The most popular touring cycling route is Galacho de Juslibol along the Ebro — circular from Zaragoza, a 17.7 miles (28.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 1 minute to complete. This route explores the unique river meanders and rich riverside vegetation of the Galacho de Juslibol. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Zaragoza South Green Ring, a moderate 21.1 miles (34.0 km) path. This route follows the Río Gállego and Ebro, offering a mix of urban and natural views. Local touring cyclists also love the Pinseque - Larralde Reservoir - Garrapinillos Loop, a 16.8 miles (27.0 km) trail leading through the countryside and past the Larralde Reservoir, often completed in about 1 hour 43 minutes. Touring cycling around Zaragoza is defined by riverside paths along the Ebro and its affluents, urban greenbelts, and excursions into the surrounding plains and hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy urban rides to more challenging excursions. The routes in Zaragoza are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 500 reviews. More than 9000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Zaragoza's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Zaragoza?
A: There are over 470 touring cycling routes around Zaragoza, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. This includes 122 easy, 236 moderate, and 113 difficult routes.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Zaragoza offers several easy touring cycling routes perfect for beginners or families. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994100283/hermitage-of-san-cristobal-from-zuera-via-the-rio-gallego" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of San Cristóbal from Zuera via the Río Gallego&lt;/a&gt;, which is 19.3 miles (31.1 km) long. Another great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994031782/pinseque-larralde-reservoir-garrapinillos-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pinseque - Larralde Reservoir - Garrapinillos Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 16.8 miles (27.0 km) trail leading through the countryside.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see on touring cycling routes in Zaragoza?
A: Zaragoza's touring cycling routes offer a diverse range of landscapes. You can enjoy picturesque paths along the Ebro River and its affluents, explore urban greenbelts, and venture into the surrounding plains and hills. Notable natural features include the unique river meanders of the Galacho de Juslibol, lush riverside vegetation, and views of the arid Los Monegros Desert in the wider region.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Zaragoza?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes around Zaragoza are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular circular route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993030953/galacho-de-juslibol-along-the-ebro-circular-from-zaragoza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Galacho de Juslibol along the Ebro — circular from Zaragoza&lt;/a&gt;, which explores unique river meanders. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993015394/parque-lineal-de-plaza-circular-along-the-canal-imperial-de-aragon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Lineal de Plaza — circular along the Canal Imperial de Aragón&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions I can see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Along your touring cycling routes, you can encounter various interesting landmarks. The city itself boasts historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4533468" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aljafería Palace&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3007697" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Basilica of Our Lady of the Pillar&lt;/a&gt;. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993025279/zaragoza-south-green-ring" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zaragoza South Green Ring&lt;/a&gt; offer urban and natural views, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993020612/camino-natural-de-la-alfranca-round-trip-from-zaragoza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Natural de La Alfranca&lt;/a&gt; provides access to natural areas.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Zaragoza?
A: The touring cycling routes in Zaragoza are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 500 reviews. Cyclists often praise the extensive network of bike lanes, the scenic riverside paths along the Ebro, and the variety of routes that cater to different ability levels, from leisurely urban rides to more challenging excursions into the countryside.

Q: Are there any routes that offer a glimpse into the region's history?
A: Yes, some routes provide historical context. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993096816/orwell-route-and-las-tres-huegas-trenches-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orwell Route and Las Tres Huegas Trenches Loop&lt;/a&gt; offers insights into historical sites. Additionally, the region around Zaragoza features numerous castles, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4013068" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alfajarín Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which can be explored as part of longer rides or day trips.

Q: Can I find routes that are more challenging or involve gravel sections?
A: For those seeking more challenging rides or gravel sections, Zaragoza offers options like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993111370/castillo-de-cornel-ermita-de-la-vigen-de-la-pena-y-toro-de-osborne-gravel-circular-from-alfajarin" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Cornel, Ermita de la Vigen de la Peña y Toro de Osborne — gravel circular from Alfajarín&lt;/a&gt;. This route includes significant elevation gain and gravel terrain, suitable for experienced touring cyclists.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Zaragoza?
A: Zaragoza generally offers good conditions for touring cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures, making them ideal for exploring. While summer can be hot, early mornings or evenings are still enjoyable. Winters are typically mild, allowing for cycling, though some routes might be less comfortable in colder, wetter conditions.

Q: Are there any routes that take me through natural reserves or unique ecological areas?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993030953/galacho-de-juslibol-along-the-ebro-circular-from-zaragoza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Galacho de Juslibol along the Ebro&lt;/a&gt; route specifically explores the unique river meanders and rich riverside vegetation of the Galacho de Juslibol, a valuable ecological space. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993382191/pueblo-viejo-de-belchite-y-reserva-ornitologica-de-el-planeron-round-trip-from-belchite" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pueblo Viejo de Belchite y Reserva Ornitologica de El Planeron&lt;/a&gt;, which includes an ornithological reserve.

Q: Are there routes that follow the Ebro River for an extended period?
A: Yes, many routes in Zaragoza leverage the Ebro River. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993035832/zaragoza-north-greenbelt" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zaragoza North Greenbelt&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993025279/zaragoza-south-green-ring" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zaragoza South Green Ring&lt;/a&gt; both follow the Ebro and its affluents, providing extensive riverside cycling opportunities with a mix of urban and natural views.
tips: Inside the Parque Lineal de Plaza we find this labyrinth. It is the Solar labyrinth, longer than the Baroque labyrinth that we have at the other end of the park, so if we want to do it we will have to add half an hour to our activity but it is surely worth doing, especially if we go with the More smalls.

A beautiful area of the Parque Lineal de Plaza where different paths will lead us to this large lake inhabited by various species of birds such as ducks or swans. Ideal for playing sports or resting and breathing nature.
